deep_dive.title: Breaking the Lock-in: Diversifying Text-to-Image Generation via Representation Modulation
deep_dive.abstract: Recent text-to-image models built on large-scale Transformer backbones and flow-based objectives deliver strong text-image alignment and high visual quality, yet often produce overly similar samples under a fixed prompt. Existing diversity-enhancement methods alleviate this issue, but typically require expensive sampling or auxiliary optimization, incurring non-trivial overhead. To investigate the root cause of this homogeneity, we examine intermediate Transformer features and observe that the zero-frequency spatial average (DC) component rapidly converges across seeds early in generation, causing early trajectory lock-in that limits downstream variation. Building on this observation, we propose DC Attenuation for diVersity Enhancement (DAVE), a training-free representation-level intervention that selectively attenuates this component in the early regime. DAVE preserves the sampling pipeline with negligible overhead, improving prompt-consistent diversity while maintaining competitive image quality.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: Modulating the DC component of intermediate Transformer features is an intervention in the latent space of deep models.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 调制中间Transformer特征的直流分量是一种深度模型潜在空间干预。
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 调制中间Transformer特征的直流分量是一种深度模型潜在空间干预。
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 调制中间Transformer特征的直流分量是一种深度模型潜在空间干预。
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: To address limited diversity in text-to-image generation, the paper modulates the zero-frequency spatial average (DC) component of intermediate Transformer features to break early trajectory lock-in, diversifying outputs without extra sampling cost.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 针对文本到图像生成中样本多样性不足的问题，本文发现中间Transformer特征的直流分量过早收敛导致生成轨迹锁定。提出DC衰减方法，通过调制该分量打破锁定，在不增加额外采样开销的情况下提升生成多样性。实验表明该方法能有效增加样本变化。该发现揭示了潜在空间干预对控制生成过程的重要性。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 针对文本到图像生成中样本多样性不足的问题，本文发现中间Transformer特征的直流分量过早收敛导致生成轨迹锁定。提出DC衰减方法，通过调制该分量打破锁定，在不增加额外采样开销的情况下提升生成多样性。实验表明该方法能有效增加样本变化。该发现揭示了潜在空间干预对控制生成过程的重要性。

deep_dive.title: When Behavioral Safety Evaluation Fails: A Representation-Level Perspective
deep_dive.abstract: Large Language Model (LLM) safety has often been evaluated at the behavior level, which provides limited evidence of internal robustness, as these evaluations target outputs rather than representation-level vulnerability under intervention. We formalize this discrepancy as the audit gap: the difference between behavioral safety and robustness under intervention. To study this gap, we construct dissociated models that preserve safe outward behavior while remaining vulnerable in the latent space. We introduce an intervention-based evaluation framework to test model robustness through soft interventions in parameter and latent spaces, including harmful fine-tuning and layer-wise latent perturbations. To formalize the evaluation, we propose the Latent Vulnerability Score (LVS) to measure how easily harmful behavior can be elicited by bounded latent perturbations. Using this evaluation framework, we show that behavioral safety metrics are insufficient measures of representation-level robustness across multiple safely and unsafely aligned state-of-the-art models. Notably, dissociated models show substantially elevated LVSs despite comparable refusal behavior under harmful intervention, with intermediate representations being the most sensitive to intervention. Our results suggest that behavioral safety evaluation alone provides an incomplete picture of model robustness, motivating representation-aware audits of latent vulnerability and observable behavior.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: Proposes an intervention-based evaluation framework with soft interventions in latent space to test model robustness.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 提出基于潜在空间干预的评估框架，通过参数和潜在空间中的软干预测试模型鲁棒性。
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 提出基于潜在空间干预的评估框架，通过参数和潜在空间中的软干预测试模型鲁棒性。
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 提出基于潜在空间干预的评估框架，通过参数和潜在空间中的软干预测试模型鲁棒性。
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: This paper formalizes the audit gap between behavioral safety and latent robustness in LLMs, and introduces an intervention-based evaluation framework using soft interventions in parameter and latent spaces to uncover representation-level vulnerabilities, even when models behave safely outwardly.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 针对大语言模型行为安全评估仅关注输出而忽略内部表示鲁棒性的缺陷，本文形式化了行为安全性与干预下鲁棒性之间的审计差距。通过构建在行为上安全但潜在空间脆弱的分离模型，提出了一种基于干预的评估框架，利用参数空间和层间潜在空间中的软干预（如有害微调和潜在扰动）来测试模型鲁棒性，并引入潜在干预鲁棒性评分进行标准化评估。该框架揭示了仅靠行为评估无法暴露的深层脆弱性，为机器学习安全评估提供了更全面的视角。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 针对大语言模型行为安全评估仅关注输出而忽略内部表示鲁棒性的缺陷，本文形式化了行为安全性与干预下鲁棒性之间的审计差距。通过构建在行为上安全但潜在空间脆弱的分离模型，提出了一种基于干预的评估框架，利用参数空间和层间潜在空间中的软干预（如有害微调和潜在扰动）来测试模型鲁棒性，并引入潜在干预鲁棒性评分进行标准化评估。该框架揭示了仅靠行为评估无法暴露的深层脆弱性，为机器学习安全评估提供了更全面的视角。

quick_skim.title: Robust-U1: Can MLLMs Self-Recover Corrupted Visual Content for Robust Understanding?
quick_skim.abstract: Multimodal Large Language Models (MLLMs) have demonstrated remarkable success in visual understanding, yet their performance degrades significantly under real-world visual corruptions. While existing robustness enhancement approaches exist, they are limited: black-box feature alignment lacks interpretability, and white-box text-based reasoning cannot restore lost pixel-level details. This work investigates a fundamental research question: Can MLLMs recover corrupted visual content by themselves? To address this, we propose Robust-U1, a novel framework that equips MLLMs with explicit visual self-recovery capability for robust understanding. The approach comprises three core stages: supervised fine-tuning for initial reconstruction, reinforcement learning with dual rewards (pixel-level SSIM and semantic-level CLIP similarity) for aligning high visual quality, and multimodal reasoning that jointly considers both the corrupted input and the recovered image. Extensive experiments demonstrate that Robust-U1 achieves state-of-the-art robustness on the real-world corruption benchmark and maintains superior performance under adversarial corruptions on general VQA benchmarks. Analysis confirms that high-quality visual recovery directly enhances reasoning performance, establishing self-recovery as a critical mechanism for robust visual understanding. The source code is available at https://github.com/jqtangust/Robust-U1.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: MLLMs self-recover corrupted visual content for robust understanding, addressing visual corruptions that include color changes.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 多模态大模型自我恢复受破坏的视觉内容以实现鲁棒理解，应对包括颜色变化在内的视觉退化。
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 多模态大模型自我恢复受破坏的视觉内容以实现鲁棒理解，应对包括颜色变化在内的视觉退化。
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 多模态大模型自我恢复受破坏的视觉内容以实现鲁棒理解，应对包括颜色变化在内的视觉退化。
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Robust-U1 enables MLLMs to self-recover corrupted visual content for robust understanding via supervised fine-tuning, reinforcement learning, and self-correction, improving performance on corrupted visual tasks.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 针对多模态大模型在视觉内容受损时理解能力下降的问题，Robust-U1框架通过监督微调、强化学习和自校正三阶段训练，使模型能够自我恢复受损视觉特征，从而在多种视觉破坏下保持语义理解准确性，提升了模型的鲁棒性。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 针对多模态大模型在视觉内容受损时理解能力下降的问题，Robust-U1框架通过监督微调、强化学习和自校正三阶段训练，使模型能够自我恢复受损视觉特征，从而在多种视觉破坏下保持语义理解准确性，提升了模型的鲁棒性。
quick_skim.matched_query_text: Papers central to 查找颜色扰动与语义保持关系, especially work that connects or combines: intervention techniques applied in the latent space of deep models; disentangling color and shape features in representations; impact of color perturbation on semantic understanding; preserving semantic meaning under color changes; maintaining semantic consistency after color modification; adversarial color attacks on semantic segmentation; papers on feature level intervention for color robustness in deep learning; methods to edit feature representations to make models robust to color changes; Methods for preserving semantic consistency under color jittering; Adversarial color perturbations and their effect on semantic robustness.

quick_skim.title: Adaptive Causal Alignment for High-Confidence Adversarial Training
quick_skim.abstract: Inverse adversarial training leverages high-confidence predictions to stabilize robust learning, yet we uncover a critical paradox: high confidence often stems from overfitting to non-causal background correlations rather than intrinsic object semantics. Our investigation reveals that visual context functions as a dual-natured signal, serving as either a necessary supportive prior or a spurious confounder. This insight renders existing blind suppression strategies flawed, as they inevitably lead to severe Feature Loss. To resolve this, we propose High-Confidence Causally Aligned Training (HICAT), a unified framework that establishes a Semantic Equilibrium. Operating on a ``Measure-Debias-Align'' pipeline, HICAT integrates a Learnable Background-Bias Estimator (LBBE) to adaptively diagnose context utility. Guided by this diagnosis, an Adaptive Debiasing mechanism performs surgical logit rectification, complemented by a geometrically grounded Foreground Logit Orthogonal Enhancement (FLOE) loss to enforce rigorous feature disentanglement. Extensive experiments on CIFAR-10, CIFAR-100, and ImageNet-1K demonstrate that HICAT consistently improves over matched baselines across diverse architectures (CNNs and ViTs) while significantly reducing the robust generalization gap.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: disentangles causal features from spurious background correlations
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 将因果特征与虚假背景相关性解耦
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 将因果特征与虚假背景相关性解耦
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 将因果特征与虚假背景相关性解耦
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Proposes a causal alignment framework for adversarial training that measures and removes spurious background biases to maintain semantic equilibrium.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 针对高置信度对抗训练中非因果背景相关性导致过拟合的问题，提出HICAT框架，通过自适应因果对齐去除虚假背景偏差并建立语义均衡。方法包含可学习的背景偏差估计器，在不损害特征的前提下提升模型对真实语义的鲁棒性。实验验证了该方法在稳定对抗训练和保持特征完整性上的有效性。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 针对高置信度对抗训练中非因果背景相关性导致过拟合的问题，提出HICAT框架，通过自适应因果对齐去除虚假背景偏差并建立语义均衡。方法包含可学习的背景偏差估计器，在不损害特征的前提下提升模型对真实语义的鲁棒性。实验验证了该方法在稳定对抗训练和保持特征完整性上的有效性。
